script:
export const meta = {
  name: 'pass3-verify',
  description: 'Pass-3: 2-agent blind verification of all 54 findings',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Verify', detail: '2 independent agents per finding (108 total)' },
    { title: 'Triage', detail: 'Collect confirmed vs rejected findings' },
  ],
}

const ROOT = '/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay'

const FINDINGS = [
  { id: 'F-001', severity: 'high', file: 'src/assay_engine/pipeline.py', line: 530, title: 'Claim fingerprint mismatch recorded but not enforced — lying claims source completes a run unchecked', description: 'After computing engine_claim_fp and source_claim_fp at pipeline.py:530-531, the boolean claim_fingerprint_matches_source is placed in the gate review payload but there is no FirewallViolation raised when they disagree. A claims source whose claim_fingerprint() returns a forged string while claims() yields real records passes through run_study without error. The companion test at test_pipeline.py:254 asserts only that the mismatch is surfaced in the payload, not that a FirewallViolation is raised.', poc: 'Create BadFingerprintClaims where claim_fingerprint() returns completely_fake_fingerprint but claims() yields a real ClaimRecord. Pass to run_study with gate_handler=auto_approve. Run completes with scorecard.n_supported=1, claim_fingerprint_matches_source: False, no FirewallViolation.' },
  { id: 'F-002', severity: 'high', file: 'src/assay_engine/methodology/confirm.py', line: 136, title: 'predicted_direction not validated at lock time or in confirm_unit_level', description: 'Hypothesis.__post_init__ does not validate predicted_direction. lock_hypothesis cryptographically binds an invalid direction verbatim. confirm_unit_level never calls _resolve_direction and never reads hypothesis.predicted_direction. A UNIT_LEVEL hypothesis locked with predicted_direction="UP" passes confirm_unit_level without any direction check.', poc: 'h = Hypothesis(..., predicted_direction="UP"), lock_hypothesis(h, authority=auth) succeeds, confirm_unit_level returns VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED with no error.' },
  { id: 'F-003', severity: 'high', file: 'src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py', line: 125, title: 'Credential scrubbing bypassed by lowercase ANTHROPIC_* env vars', description: 'is_metered_anthropic_credential() does case-sensitive startswith("ANTHROPIC_") check. anthropic_api_key (lowercase) is not scrubbed. _UNSAFE_SUBPROCESS_VARS uses key.upper() for AWS/GOOGLE but is_metered_anthropic_credential does not.', poc: 'Set anthropic_api_key=[REDACTED]. Call scrubbed_env(). Key is not scrubbed.' },
  { id: 'F-004', severity: 'high', file: '.github/workflows/ci.yml', line: 24, title: 'CI test lanes install from live PyPI without lockfile hash enforcement', description: 'core and integration jobs use pip install -e without requirements.lock or --require-hashes. The audit job validates the lockfile and runs pip-audit but does NOT run the test suite. A malicious package version injected into PyPI would execute inside the test environment.', poc: 'Attacker publishes malicious patch version within pyproject.toml ranges. Integration job installs compromised version. Audit job checks requirements.lock (pinned to old clean version) and passes.' },
  { id: 'F-005', severity: 'high', file: 'src/assay_engine/observability/tracing.py', line: 198, title: 'OtelTracer.span does not set ERROR status or record exception on the span when body raises', description: 'OtelTracer.span uses tracer.start_as_current_span(name) with a bare yield and no error-handling. The OTel Python SDK does NOT automatically call span.set_status(StatusCode.ERROR) or span.record_exception(exc) for the context-manager form used here. Every FirewallViolation, IngestionError, GateError leaves the enclosing phase span in UNSET status in Phoenix.', poc: 'Run a study that raises IngestionError inside with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"). Inspect the exported span — status is UNSET, not ERROR.' },
  { id: 'F-006', severity: 'high', file: 'src/assay_engine/pipeline.py', line: 315, title: 'Successful run has no terminal run_end provenance entry', description: 'Provenance trail records run_start and run_failed but has no run_end/run_complete entry on the SUCCESS path. An auditor cannot distinguish a trail that ended normally from one truncated mid-run.', poc: 'Call run_study successfully. Inspect result.provenance[-1].kind — it is "report" (phase entry), not "run_end".' },
  { id: 'F-007', severity: 'high', file: 'src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py', line: 89, title: 'Gate decisions silently omit the provenance trail when recorder=None', description: 'make_gate_node takes optional recorder defaulting to None. When omitted, gate decisions recorded only as LangGraph graph state, NOT hash-chained, NOT tamper-evident. The default makes the insecure configuration effortless to accidentally ship.', poc: 'Call make_gate_node(gate, propose) without recorder. Run a study. Trail has no gate entries. Edit LangGraph checkpoint to flip rejected to approved; verify_records passes.' },
  { id: 'F-008', severity: 'high', file: 'src/assay_engine/pipeline.py', line: 368, title: 'versioner.put() failure raises raw untyped exception instead of IngestionError', description: 'versioner.put(str(plan.source)) at line 368 is called without error handling. A storage failure propagates as a raw untyped exception, not IngestionError as documented.', poc: 'Pass LocalDataVersioner pointing at read-only directory. Call run_study. Raises PermissionError with no IngestionError wrapping and no trail entry.' },
  { id: 'F-009', severity: 'high', file: 'src/assay_engine/pipeline.py', line: 380, title: 'assert statements guarding critical plan fields disabled under python -O', description: 'Five assert statements guard fields that StudyPlan.__post_init__ already validates. When Python runs with -O, all asserts disabled. If plan fields mutated after construction, AttributeError raised instead of FirewallViolation.', poc: 'Run Python with -O. Monkey-patch plan.split = None after construction. Call run_study. Assert at line 440 skipped; line 442 raises AttributeError.' },
  { id: 'F-010', severity: 'high', file: 'src/assay_engine/methodology/adjudication.py', line: 103, title: 'adjudicate() standalone function silently produces vacuous scorecard when claims_source yields no claims', description: 'adjudicate() does not check for empty claims. Pipeline run_study checks and raises ValueError. The public standalone function lacks this guard.', poc: 'MockClaimsSource whose claims() returns iter([]). Call adjudicate(corpus, mock_source). Returns SourceScorecard(total=0, alignment_rate=None) with no error.' },
  { id: 'F-011', severity: 'high', file: 'src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py', line: 434, title: 'anyio.run() inside _high_stakes_complete called from ThreadPoolExecutor — RuntimeError if invoked from existing event loop', description: '_high_stakes_complete() calls anyio.run(_drain) at line 434. If ever invoked while an asyncio event loop is running (LangGraph async node, asyncio.run_in_executor), anyio.run() raises RuntimeError.', poc: 'async def bad(): _high_stakes_complete(...); asyncio.run(bad()) — RuntimeError.' },
  { id: 'F-012', severity: 'high', file: '.github/workflows/ci.yml', line: 116, title: 'SBOM generation swallows failure with || true — broken/absent SBOM uploaded silently', description: 'SBOM step runs pip-audit ... -o sbom.cyclonedx.json || true. Failure masked. Upload step has if-no-files-found: warn so absent SBOM produces only a warning; CI stays green.', poc: 'Inject pip-audit error. CI passes with warning but no blocking failure.' },
  { id: 'F-013', severity: 'high', file: '.github/workflows/ci.yml', line: 131, title: 'License gate scans live-resolved dependency set, not pinned lockfile', description: 'Audit job license gate installs with pip install -e ".[all]" (live resolver), not requirements.lock. Installed set may differ from what pip-audit scanned.', poc: 'A dependency resolves to newer AGPL version in live resolver. License gate flags it but pip-audit passes (checked the locked clean version).' },
  { id: 'F-014', severity: 'high', file: '.github/workflows/ci.yml', line: 8, title: 'No umbrella job — audit and integration jobs not guaranteed merge-gate checks', description: 'Three independent jobs with no needs: dependencies and no umbrella fan-in job. Branch protection must list each job by name. Easy to misconfigure and skip audit job.', poc: 'Configure branch protection to require only core. Push PR with vulnerable dep. core passes; audit fails. PR is mergeable.' },
  { id: 'F-015', severity: 'high', file: 'scripts/license_gate.py', line: 15, title: 'License gate misses SSPL and EUPL full-name variants', description: '_DENY = ("GPL", "AGPL", "SSPL", "EUPL") only matches token substrings. "Server Side Public License" does not contain "SSPL"; "European Union Public Licence 1.2" does not contain "EUPL". Both bypass the gate.', poc: '"SSPL" in "SERVER SIDE PUBLIC LICENSE".upper() -> False. Both families pass through undetected.' },
  { id: 'F-016', severity: 'high', file: 'src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py', line: 44, title: 'corpus_fingerprint() materialises entire corpus into RAM with no streaming path', description: 'corpus_fingerprint() sorts corpus.units and relations into lists, builds O(U) dicts, then passes to json.dumps(). For 100k-unit corpus doubles peak memory. Also called twice per run_study.', poc: 'For 100k units, two calls happen: pipeline.py:365 and determinism.py:115 inside the builder. Full corpus JSON built in RAM twice.' },
  { id: 'F-017', severity: 'high', file: 'src/assay_engine/methodology/confirm.py', line: 190, title: 'O(R * N) resample stability computation in pure Python — no vectorisation path', description: '_resample_stability() calls _empirical_p(null, r, tail) once per resample statistic. _empirical_p iterates full null_distribution O(N) per call. R=1000, N=10000 -> 10M Python comparisons. No numpy path.', poc: 'R=1000, N=10000: 10 million Python comparisons in stability loop alone.' },
  { id: 'F-018', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/methodology/discovery.py', line: 108, title: 'discover_and_confirm missing duplicate hypothesis_id guard', description: 'Pass-2 #15 added claim_id guards in adjudicate_with_baseline and _require_unique_ids for pipeline. But discover_and_confirm (standalone public) was not updated. Duplicate hypothesis_ids pass without error.', poc: 'discover_and_confirm(..., discover=lambda c: [locked_h, locked_h]) returns two verdicts for same hypothesis_id.' },
  { id: 'F-019', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/methodology/adjudication.py', line: 197, title: 'adjudicate_with_baseline guards duplicate claim_ids but not duplicate hypothesis_ids', description: 'Two claims with distinct claim_ids whose hypothesis_for both return same hypothesis_id both pass. Scorecard alignment_rate inflated; verdict counts double-count.', poc: 'Two ClaimRecords with distinct ids whose hypothesis_for both return Hypothesis(hypothesis_id="H-shared"): scorecard.total=2, n_supported=2 without error.' },
  { id: 'F-020', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py', line: 158, title: '_close_all_pools() iterates _OPEN_POOLS without holding _init_lock', description: '_close_all_pools() (atexit handler) iterates _OPEN_POOLS with no lock. _register_pool_cleanup() writes under _init_lock. On GIL-free Python 3.13, concurrent list.append reallocation during iteration could crash.', poc: 'GIL-free Python 3.13 free-threaded build: thread calling _register_pool_cleanup concurrently with _close_all_pools triggers use-after-free.' },
  { id: 'F-021', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/pipeline.py', line: 498, title: 'confirm_held_out or confirm_claim returning None raises AttributeError instead of FirewallViolation', description: 'verdict = plan.confirm_held_out(h, held_out) not validated as Verdict before accessing verdict.hypothesis_id. None return causes AttributeError, not typed error.', poc: 'plan.confirm_held_out = lambda h, corpus: None. Run run_study in DISCOVERY mode. Raises AttributeError.' },
  { id: 'F-022', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py', line: 608, title: 'run_json overall deadline bounds only backoff sleeps, not actual LLM call time', description: 'RUN_JSON_DEADLINE enforced only during backoff sleep intervals, not in-progress backend calls. Worst case: deadline + retries * per-call-timeout far exceeds documented deadline.', poc: 'ASSAY_BULK_TIMEOUT=120, ASSAY_REASONING_RETRIES=2, ASSAY_JSON_REROLLS=2, ASSAY_RUN_JSON_DEADLINE=10. Backend stalls for 130s per call. run_json takes ~390s despite 10s deadline.' },
  { id: 'F-023', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py', line: 70, title: 'MlflowExperimentTracker.start_run partial failure orphans MLflow run in RUNNING state', description: 'create_run() creates MLflow run then log_param() called in loop. If log_param raises, run_id never ends the run. MLflow run permanently in RUNNING state.', poc: 'Mock log_param to raise. Call run_study with MlflowExperimentTracker. Orphaned run stays RUNNING with no end time.' },
  { id: 'F-024', severity: 'medium', file: 'transcripts/sessions/8d54714a-d616-4911-a386-9b4b0f2ce74d.jsonl', line: 1, title: 'Operator PII (username, filesystem paths) committed to git history in transcripts/', description: 'transcripts/ directory (1422 files) committed to git history, contains local filesystem paths, operator username (6818 occurrences in one session file), and email. Not in .gitignore. DEFAULT_SRC hardcoded to hc-GRC project Claude session directory.', poc: 'grep -c [REDACTED] transcripts/sessions/8d54714a-*.jsonl returns 6818 matches.' },
  { id: 'F-025', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py', line: 84, title: '_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS is written but never read as a guard — dead module-level state', description: '_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS populated at line 271 inside get_checkpointer() but never consulted. Actual idempotency guard is _cached_pool(conn_str) checking _POOLS_BY_CONN. Dead variable complicates reasoning.', poc: 'Remove _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str) and run full test suite — all tests pass.' },
  { id: 'F-026', severity: 'medium', file: 'tests/test_persistence.py', line: 476, title: 'Concurrent test and single-threaded tests mutate os.environ directly without monkeypatch', description: 'Tests call os.environ["ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL"] = ... directly, bypassing monkeypatch. Single-threaded tests leave ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL permanently mutated, polluting subsequent tests.', poc: 'Run two tests sequentially that set os.environ directly. Second test sees first test\'s value if monkeypatch.delenv not called.' },
  { id: 'F-027', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/pipeline.py', line: 342, title: 'bootstrap_tracing() called after OtelTracer construction — global provider may not be set when first span is emitted', description: 'bootstrap_tracing() called at line 342 AFTER tracer = tracer or OtelTracer() at line 274. If custom tracer emits spans in constructor before bootstrap_tracing() returns, those spans go to pre-registration no-op provider.', poc: 'Pass custom tracer that emits span in constructor. That span goes to the pre-registration no-op provider.' },
  { id: 'F-028', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/pipeline.py', line: 324, title: 'MLflow run_id is None when start_run fails — all metrics silently dropped with no structured warning', description: 'If tracker.start_run() raises, run_id remains None. track() helper silently returns early. ALL metrics (n_supported, alignment_rate, etc.) silently dropped. No warning emitted.', poc: 'Pass MlflowExperimentTracker pointing at non-existent SQLite path. Complete successful study. result.experiment_run_id is None and no metrics logged, no warning.' },
  { id: 'F-029', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/pipeline.py', line: 515, title: 'No SLO-relevant metrics tracked: run duration, corpus size, and claim count absent', description: 'Tracker logs alignment_rate and verdict counts but never logs: run wall-clock duration, corpus size (n_units), claim count (n_claims), baseline build time. Cannot detect slow baselines or runaway calls.', poc: 'Run 10 studies of increasing corpus size. Query MLflow store — no n_units, n_claims, or run_duration_s metrics.' },
  { id: 'F-030', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/pipeline.py', line: 619, title: 'Exception inside trail.record("run_failed") silently swallowed — failure reason lost', description: 'If trail.record() raises during run_failed recording, inner except Exception: pass swallows it. Failure reason lost from provenance trail with no logging.', poc: 'Inject ProvenanceTrail subclass whose record() always raises on kind="run_failed". Run study that raises. Trail ends at last phase entry; exception type and phase not recoverable.' },
  { id: 'F-031', severity: 'medium', file: '.github/workflows/ci.yml', line: 91, title: 'uv installed unpinned in lockfile sync check — version change causes false-positive diffs', description: 'Lockfile sync step installs uv with pip install --upgrade uv, always fetching latest. If uv changes lockfile output format between version used to generate requirements.lock and CI version, spurious diffs block all PRs.', poc: 'Pin requirements.lock to uv 0.4.x. Release uv 0.5 with changed annotation format. CI sync check fails on every PR.' },
  { id: 'F-032', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py', line: 207, title: '_inflight counter leak if KeyboardInterrupt fires between _pool.submit() and add_done_callback()', description: 'In _submit_bounded, _inflight incremented before _pool.submit(). After submit() returns but before add_done_callback(), a KeyboardInterrupt can fire. _release never registered. After 8 such leaks, RateLimitError on every call.', poc: 'Signal handler raises KeyboardInterrupt timed to fire between submit and add_done_callback. _inflight increments without decrementing.' },
  { id: 'F-033', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py', line: 59, title: '_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS captured at module import time — late env var set silently disables guard', description: '_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS set at module import. If ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS set after import, running value stays import-time value. Also _MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS=0 code path completely untested.', poc: 'Set ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS=0 after module import. _acquire_migration_lock() uses import-time value (30000).' },
  { id: 'F-034', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/_frozen.py', line: 50, title: 'freeze_mapping() is not idempotent — re-freezing an already-frozen FrozenDict discards cached hash', description: 'freeze_mapping() always allocates new FrozenDict with fresh _hash = None, discarding cached hash from original. In run_study(), ProvenanceEntry.__post_init__ re-freezes payloads, causing unnecessary O(N*depth) hash recomputation.', poc: 'freeze_mapping on already-frozen FrozenDict: new object _hash is None. Next hash() call does full O(N*depth) traversal.' },
  { id: 'F-035', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/contracts/features.py', line: 37, title: 'FeatureMatrix.__post_init__ O(n*d) pure-Python cell validation — 10M isinstance() calls for 100k-unit corpus', description: '__post_init__ validates every cell with nested Python loop: isinstance() then math.isfinite(). For U=100k, D=100: 10M isinstance() + 10M isfinite() calls at construction time.', poc: 'Construct FeatureMatrix with 100k rows of 100 floats. __post_init__ takes seconds on CPython.' },
  { id: 'F-036', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/pipeline.py', line: 617, title: 'Provenance chain full re-verification at end of every run — O(N) redundant SHA-256/HMAC recomputes', description: 'run_study() calls trail.verify() unconditionally. verify_records() iterates every entry and recomputes digest from scratch. 1500 entries triggers 1500 HMAC-SHA256 operations redundantly.', poc: 'Run with 1500 trail entries. trail.verify() triggers 1500 JSON serialisations + 1500 SHA-256 ops at run end, all redundant for in-memory trail.' },
  { id: 'F-037', severity: 'medium', file: '.github/workflows/ci.yml', line: 17, title: 'GitHub Actions pinned to mutable version tags, not commit SHAs', description: 'All Actions pinned to @v4, @v5 tags rather than immutable commit SHAs. A compromised tag would silently execute malicious code in CI.', poc: 'Attacker force-pushes v4 tag to commit with exfiltration code. Every CI run referencing @v4 executes malicious step.' },
  { id: 'F-038', severity: 'medium', file: 'tests/test_docs_drift.py', line: 25, title: 'test_data_sovereignty_is_qualified_for_high_stakes_tier uses loose "off-box" keyword — one-sided guard', description: 'Test asserts "off-box" in text for GOVERNANCE.md. If high-stakes caveat deleted while another "off-box" occurrence remains, test passes. Also test_baseline_toolkit_scope_is_honest never asserts stale embedding/clustering claims are absent.', poc: 'Remove only high-stakes caveat from GOVERNANCE.md (keep SaaS observability sentence). Test passes. Add stale embedding builders back alongside BaselineBuilder in ARCHITECTURE.md. Test passes.' },
  { id: 'F-039', severity: 'medium', file: 'tests/test_supply_chain.py', line: 133, title: 'test_license_gate_present is string-presence check, not behavioral test — SSPL/EUPL bypass undetected', description: 'Test reads license_gate.py source and asserts "AGPL" and "GPL" in text. Does not import and call license_gate.main() with synthetic JSON. SSPL/EUPL bypass (F-015) not caught.', poc: 'Delete "SSPL" and "EUPL" from _DENY in license_gate.py. test_license_gate_present still passes.' },
  { id: 'F-040', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py', line: 244, title: 'Connection pool max_size hardcoded with no env-var override', description: 'ConnectionPool constructed with hardcoded min_size=1, max_size=8. No ASSAY_POOL_MAX_SIZE env var. Reasoning pool already uses 8 workers; simultaneous load saturates all slots. No connect_timeout either.', poc: 'Point at TCP black-hole. get_checkpointer() hangs for up to 30s before raising.' },
  { id: 'F-041', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/pipeline.py', line: 442, title: 'subset_corpus() called up to four times in discovery mode — triple/quad corpus traversal', description: 'run_study calls subset_corpus twice. If adapter discover callable internally calls discover_and_confirm(), that calls subset_corpus again (twice). Four O(U+R) traversals for same partition.', poc: 'pipeline.py:442-443 calls subset_corpus twice. If discover() calls discover_and_confirm(): discovery.py:99 and :110 each call subset_corpus again — four total traversals.' },
  { id: 'F-042', severity: 'medium', file: 'src/assay_engine/pipeline.py', line: 449, title: 'Empty discover() return silently accepted — vacuous discovery run completes with zero verdicts', description: 'hypotheses = list(plan.discover(discovery_corpus)) may return empty list. No guard raises error. Run completes with discovery_verdicts=(), all phases recorded. Cannot distinguish from silently broken discover callable.', poc: 'plan.discover = lambda corpus: []. run_study returns StudyResult with discovery_verdicts=() and no error.' },
  { id: 'F-043', severity: 'low', file: 'src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py', line: 181, title: 'Module-level ThreadPoolExecutor _pool has no atexit shutdown', description: '_pool created at module import with no atexit.register(_pool.shutdown). In-flight futures at interpreter exit may be torn down mid-call, potentially corrupting Ollama connection pool state.', poc: 'Fill all 8 slots with slow tasks, trigger interpreter exit. Background workers may be torn down mid-HTTP-call.' },
  { id: 'F-044', severity: 'low', file: 'src/assay_engine/observability/tracing.py', line: 84, title: 'bootstrap_tracing() silently swallows all setup failures with no logging', description: 'except Exception: return None catches all failures during Phoenix registration. No log entry emitted. Misconfigured ASSAY_TRACING_HOST silently runs untraced.', poc: 'Set ASSAY_TRACING_HOST=nonexistent-host. Call bootstrap_tracing(). Returns None, no log output.' },
  { id: 'F-045', severity: 'low', file: 'src/assay_engine/provenance.py', line: 206, title: 'to_records()/from_records() have no persistence integration — hash-chained trail lost on process exit', description: 'run_study never calls to_records() or persists the trail to disk or MLflow. Trail survives only as in-memory StudyResult.provenance. Lost on process exit if caller does not serialize.', poc: 'Call run_study and let process exit without serializing result.provenance. Trail is gone. MLflow store has nothing.' },
  { id: 'F-046', severity: 'low', file: 'src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py', line: 108, title: 'Gate node re-entry can double-call recorder for same decision', description: 'ProvenanceTrail.record() is NOT idempotent — a double-call appends duplicate entry. If parallel branch races between apply_gate_decision and recorder(rec), recorder can be called twice for same decision.', poc: 'Wire two parallel gate nodes sharing same ProvenanceTrail recorder. Both call recorder after getting approved. Trail receives two "gate" entries with same gate name.' },
  { id: 'F-047', severity: 'low', file: 'tests/test_persistence.py', line: 439, title: 'FakeConn mock does not record pg_advisory_unlock — explicit unlock call is completely untested', description: 'FakeConn.execute() mock only records lock_timeout and pg_advisory_lock calls, not pg_advisory_unlock. If unlock call removed, no test catches it.', poc: 'Remove pg_advisory_unlock call from get_checkpointer(). Run test suite — all tests pass.' },
  { id: 'F-048', severity: 'low', file: 'examples/minimal_study.py', line: 36, title: 'Hardcoded HMAC secret in example with no rotation warning', description: 'LocalHmacAuthority(b"example-study-secret-key-00000001") with only a comment. No runtime guard against using this exact value in production. PAST = datetime.now() - timedelta(hours=1) creates backdated registration.', poc: 'Any party knowing this public secret can craft valid local-hmac:v1: proof for arbitrary hypothesis content.' },
  { id: 'F-049', severity: 'low', file: 'src/assay_engine/pipeline.py', line: 255, title: 'Provenance trail is unkeyed (forgery-vulnerable) by default when secret=None', description: 'run_study accepts secret: bytes | None = None. Unkeyed trail is NOT forgery-resistant: adversary can edit any entry payload, recompute full SHA-256 chain, and produce trail that passes verify_records(). Default is insecure with no warning.', poc: 'Call run_study without secret. Edit entry 1 payload. Recompute chain from genesis. verify_records(entries) with secret=None passes.' },
  { id: 'F-050', severity: 'low', file: 'scripts/license_gate.py', line: 19, title: 'Unclosed file handle in license_gate.py', description: 'packages = json.load(open(path, encoding="utf-8")) opens file without context manager. File handle not closed until GC runs.', poc: 'In constrained environment, leaked file descriptors exhaust process limit.' },
  { id: 'F-051', severity: 'low', file: 'scripts/capture_transcripts.py', line: 39, title: 'Bearer token scrubbing regex misses base64 characters (+ and /) — partial redaction leaves token fragments', description: 'Bearer token redaction pattern [A-Za-z0-9._\\-]{20,} missing + and /. Base64 tokens split at first + or /, trailing portion remains in transcript.', poc: '[REDACTED]: retains +iOiJIUzI1NiJ9.payload/signature after redaction.' },
  { id: 'F-052', severity: 'low', file: 'tests/test_supply_chain.py', line: 148, title: 'Behavioral allowlist tests exercise hardcoded copy of parse loop, not actual ci.yml — filename regression undetected', description: 'Tests execute hardcoded _PARSE_LOOP against tmp_path file, not ci.yml. If ci.yml changed allowlist filename from .pip-audit-ignore, behavioral tests would still pass.', poc: 'Change .pip-audit-ignore to .pip-audit-allowlist in ci.yml. All supply chain tests pass.' },
  { id: 'F-053', severity: 'low', file: 'tests/test_supply_chain.py', line: 125, title: 'test_dependabot_monitors_dependencies does not assert GitHub Actions ecosystem is covered', description: 'Test verifies only package-ecosystem: pip appears in dependabot.yml. If github-actions block deleted, test passes. Action-pinning drift goes unmonitored.', poc: 'Delete github-actions block from dependabot.yml. test_dependabot_monitors_dependencies passes.' },

const VERDICT_SCHEMA = {
  type: 'object',
  required: ['finding_id', 'confirmed', 'reasoning', 'severity_assessment'],
  properties: {
    finding_id: { type: 'string' },
    confirmed: { type: 'boolean' },
    reasoning: { type: 'string' },
    severity_assessment: { type: 'string', enum: ['critical', 'high', 'medium', 'low', 'false_positive'] },
  }
}

phase('Verify')
const verifyPrompt = (f, verifierNum) =>
  `You are verifier #${verifierNum}, independent of all other verifiers. Do NOT confirm findings optimistically — only confirm if you can independently verify the issue exists in the actual code at ${ROOT}.

Finding ${f.id}: "${f.title}"
File: ${f.file} (line ${f.line})
Description: ${f.description}
PoC: ${f.poc}

Instructions:
1. Read the actual file at ${ROOT}/${f.file}
2. Look at the code at line ${f.line} and surrounding context
3. Independently verify whether the described problem EXISTS in the current code
4. Check if the PoC is reproducible from the actual code as written
5. Consider whether this is already fixed (pass-1 #101-#122, pass-2 #124-#156 fixed many issues)
6. Return your independent verdict

Confirmed = true ONLY if: (a) the code problem exists as described, (b) the PoC is reproducible from current code, (c) it is a genuine production-readiness concern. Set confirmed=false for speculative issues, issues already fixed, or issues where the PoC does not match what the code actually does.`

const results = await pipeline(
  FINDINGS,
  finding => parallel([
    () => agent(verifyPrompt(finding, 1), { label: 'verify-A:' + finding.id, phase: 'Verify', schema: VERDICT_SCHEMA }),
    () => agent(verifyPrompt(finding, 2), { label: 'verify-B:' + finding.id, phase: 'Verify', schema: VERDICT_SCHEMA }),
  ]),
  (verdicts, finding) => {
    const [v1, v2] = verdicts.filter(Boolean)
    const bothConfirm = v1 && v2 && v1.confirmed && v2.confirmed
    const neitherConfirm = (!v1 || !v1.confirmed) && (!v2 || !v2.confirmed)
    return {
      finding_id: finding.id,
      title: finding.title,
      severity: finding.severity,
      file: finding.file,
      line: finding.line,
      suggested_fix: finding.poc,
      verdict_a: v1,
      verdict_b: v2,
      both_confirmed: bothConfirm,
      neither_confirmed: neitherConfirm,
      split: !bothConfirm && !neitherConfirm,
    }
  }
)

const confirmed = results.filter(Boolean).filter(r => r.both_confirmed)
const rejected = results.filter(Boolean).filter(r => r.neither_confirmed)
const split_verdicts = results.filter(Boolean).filter(r => r.split)

log('Confirmed (both agents agree): ' + confirmed.length)
log('Rejected (neither agent confirms): ' + rejected.length)
log('Split verdicts (1 of 2 confirms): ' + split_verdicts.length)

return {
  confirmed,
  rejected,
  split_verdicts,
  all_results: results.filter(Boolean),
}

confirmed.title: predicted_direction not validated at lock time or in confirm_unit_level
confirmed.suggested_fix: h = Hypothesis(..., predicted_direction="UP"), lock_hypothesis(h, authority=auth) succeeds, confirm_unit_level returns VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED with no error.

It performs no validation of predicted_direction. Constructing a Hypothesis with predicted_direction="UP" succeeds without error, and lock_hypothesis will cryptographically bind that invalid value verbatim.

2. confirm_unit_level (confirm.py, lines 136-165) never calls _resolve_direction and never reads hypothesis.predicted_direction. It accepts a caller-supplied direction_supports_claim boolean and passes it directly to verdict_from_pvalue. The pre-registered predicted_direction on the hypothesis is completely ignored for UNIT_LEVEL confirmation.

3. _resolve_direction (confirm.py, lines 197-225) does validate directions against _VALID_DIRECTIONS = ("greater", "less") and would reject "UP" — but this function is only called from confirm_whole_corpus (line 270), not from confirm_unit_level. The asymmetry is real and current.

The PoC is reproducible from the code as written: h = Hypothesis(..., predicted_direction="UP") succeeds, lock_hypothesis(h, authority=auth) succeeds binding "UP" verbatim, and confirm_unit_level returns a verdict determined solely by the caller's direction_supports_claim flag with no check against the pre-registered direction. This is a genuine production-readiness concern: UNIT_LEVEL hypotheses can be confirmed with an invalid or post-hoc direction because the enforcement that exists for WHOLE_CORPUS hypotheses was never wired into the unit-level path.

verdict_b.reasoning:
The code confirms all three aspects of the finding:

1. Hypothesis.__post_init__ (line 80-83 of hypothesis.py) validates only source_claim_id for EXTERNAL_CLAIM origins. predicted_direction is never validated — "UP" or any other invalid string is accepted without error.

2. lock_hypothesis in preregistration.py calls canonical_hypothesis_digest which includes predicted_direction as-is (line 117: "predicted_direction": hypothesis.predicted_direction). No validation occurs. An invalid direction is cryptographically bound verbatim.

3. confirm_unit_level (lines 136-165 of confirm.py) does NOT call _resolve_direction. It accepts direction_supports_claim as a caller-supplied bool and passes it directly to verdict_from_pvalue. The hypothesis.predicted_direction field is never read or validated inside confirm_unit_level.

The PoC is fully reproducible from the current code: Hypothesis(..., predicted_direction="UP", kind=HypothesisKind.UNIT_LEVEL) will construct without error, lock_hypothesis will succeed (binding "UP" into the proof), and confirm_unit_level will return VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED (or any verdict) based only on the caller-supplied direction_supports_claim, never detecting the invalid direction.

The _resolve_direction function at lines 197-225 was specifically written to catch unrecognized directions ("an unrecognized tail would silently flip the supported/contradicted verdict") but is only wired into confirm_whole_corpus, leaving the UNIT_LEVEL path unprotected. The pre-registration guarantee that direction cannot be chosen post-hoc is therefore not enforced for UNIT_LEVEL hypotheses.

There is no call to sp.set_status(StatusCode.ERROR) or sp.record_exception(exc) anywhere in the method. The OTel Python SDK's start_as_current_span context manager does not auto-set ERROR status or record exceptions unless the caller passes record_exception=True and set_status_on_exception=True kwargs (which this code does not do). Therefore any exception (IngestionError, FirewallViolation, GateError, etc.) raised inside a `with tracer.span(...)` block will propagate through the bare yield, leaving the span with UNSET status in Phoenix. confirmed.title: _inflight counter leak if KeyboardInterrupt fires between _pool.submit() and add_done_callback()
confirmed.suggested_fix: Signal handler raises KeyboardInterrupt timed to fire between submit and add_done_callback. _inflight increments without decrementing.

verdict_a.reasoning:
The vulnerability exists exactly as described. In `_submit_bounded` at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py:

1. `_inflight += 1` occurs at line 205 inside the lock block.
2. The `try/except BaseException` block covers only `_pool.submit(fn)` (lines 206-215). This correctly decrements `_inflight` if `submit()` raises.
3. However, line 216 (`future.add_done_callback(_release)`) is OUTSIDE the try block. If a `KeyboardInterrupt` fires after `_pool.submit()` returns successfully (completing the `future = _pool.submit(fn)` assignment) but before `future.add_done_callback(_release)` executes, the exception propagates up the call stack with `_release` never registered and `_inflight` never decremented.

In CPython, `KeyboardInterrupt` is delivered between bytecode instructions. The assignment and the subsequent `add_done_callback` call are separate bytecode sequences, so the race window is real. With `_POOL_WORKERS` slots (default 8), 8 such interrupts would permanently saturate `_inflight`, causing every subsequent call to raise `RateLimitError("reasoning timeout pool saturated")`. The fix would be to wrap both `_pool.submit()` and `future.add_done_callback(_release)` in the same try/except, or restructure so the callback is registered before releasing the guard.

verdict_b.reasoning:
The vulnerability exists exactly as described. In _submit_bounded (/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py):

1. Line 205: _inflight is incremented inside the _inflight_lock block.
2. Lines 206-215: A try/except BaseException block wraps only _pool.submit(fn). If submit raises (including KeyboardInterrupt during submit), the except BaseException at line 212 correctly decrements _inflight.
3. Line 216: future.add_done_callback(_release) is OUTSIDE the try/except block.

The race window exists between the successful return of _pool.submit(fn) (line 207) and the execution of future.add_done_callback(_release) (line 216). If KeyboardInterrupt fires in this window, the except BaseException handler has already exited scope. The _release callback is never registered, the future completes without decrementing _inflight, and the slot leaks permanently. After _POOL_WORKERS (8) such leaks, every subsequent call raises RateLimitError with "reasoning timeout pool saturated".

The fix is to move add_done_callback inside the try block, or wrap lines 207-216 in a single try/except BaseException that handles both submit failure and the post-submit interrupt window.

confirmed.title: Bearer token scrubbing regex misses base64 characters (+ and /) — partial redaction leaves token fragments
confirmed.suggested_fix: [REDACTED]: retains +iOiJIUzI1NiJ9.payload/signature after redaction.

verdict_a.reasoning: Line 39 of /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/capture_transcripts.py contains: re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-]{20,}"). The character class [A-Za-z0-9._\-] omits + and /, which are standard base64 alphabet characters. JWTs and many OAuth bearer tokens encode their payload in base64url or standard base64 and frequently contain these characters. The regex match stops at the first + or / encountered, so the portion of the token after that character is left unredacted in the captured transcript. The PoC is directly reproducible from the code as written: a token like eyJhbGc+iOiJIUzI1NiJ9.payload/signature would result in only "Bearer eyJhbGc" being replaced with [REDACTED], leaving "+iOiJIUzI1NiJ9.payload/signature" exposed. This is a genuine production-readiness concern because transcripts are committed to the repo by the pre-commit hook, and partial token fragments can still be exploitable or used to reconstruct secrets. The issue does not appear to have been fixed in prior hardening passes — the pattern is still literally [A-Za-z0-9._\-]{20,} with no + or / included.

verdict_b.reasoning: Line 39 of /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/capture_transcripts.py contains the pattern `re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-]{20,}")`. The character class `[A-Za-z0-9._\-]` excludes `+` and `/`, both of which are valid base64 characters that appear in JWTs and standard base64-encoded bearer tokens. The PoC is reproducible and the behavior is actually worse than described: if the base64 token segment before the first `+` or `/` is fewer than 20 characters (common in JWTs where `eyJhbGc` is only 7 chars before a `+`), the entire token fails to match the `{20,}` minimum and is left completely unredacted in the transcript. all_results.title: _inflight counter leak if KeyboardInterrupt fires between _pool.submit() and add_done_callback()
all_results.suggested_fix: Signal handler raises KeyboardInterrupt timed to fire between submit and add_done_callback. _inflight increments without decrementing.

verdict_a.reasoning:
The vulnerability exists exactly as described. In `_submit_bounded` at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py:

1. `_inflight += 1` occurs at line 205 inside the lock block.
2. The `try/except BaseException` block covers only `_pool.submit(fn)` (lines 206-215). This correctly decrements `_inflight` if `submit()` raises.
3. However, line 216 (`future.add_done_callback(_release)`) is OUTSIDE the try block. If a `KeyboardInterrupt` fires after `_pool.submit()` returns successfully (completing the `future = _pool.submit(fn)` assignment) but before `future.add_done_callback(_release)` executes, the exception propagates up the call stack with `_release` never registered and `_inflight` never decremented.

In CPython, `KeyboardInterrupt` is delivered between bytecode instructions. The assignment and the subsequent `add_done_callback` call are separate bytecode sequences, so the race window is real. With `_POOL_WORKERS` slots (default 8), 8 such interrupts would permanently saturate `_inflight`, causing every subsequent call to raise `RateLimitError("reasoning timeout pool saturated")`. The fix would be to wrap both `_pool.submit()` and `future.add_done_callback(_release)` in the same try/except, or restructure so the callback is registered before releasing the guard.

verdict_b.reasoning:
The vulnerability exists exactly as described. In _submit_bounded (/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py):

1. Line 205: _inflight is incremented inside the _inflight_lock block.
2. Lines 206-215: A try/except BaseException block wraps only _pool.submit(fn). If submit raises (including KeyboardInterrupt during submit), the except BaseException at line 212 correctly decrements _inflight.
3. Line 216: future.add_done_callback(_release) is OUTSIDE the try/except block.

The race window exists between the successful return of _pool.submit(fn) (line 207) and the execution of future.add_done_callback(_release) (line 216). If KeyboardInterrupt fires in this window, the except BaseException handler has already exited scope. The _release callback is never registered, the future completes without decrementing _inflight, and the slot leaks permanently. After _POOL_WORKERS (8) such leaks, every subsequent call raises RateLimitError with "reasoning timeout pool saturated".

The fix is to move add_done_callback inside the try block, or wrap lines 207-216 in a single try/except BaseException that handles both submit failure and the post-submit interrupt window.

all_results.title: freeze_mapping() is not idempotent — re-freezing an already-frozen FrozenDict discards cached hash
all_results.suggested_fix: freeze_mapping on already-frozen FrozenDict: new object _hash is None. Next hash() call does full O(N*depth) traversal.

verdict_a.reasoning: The bug exists in the current code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/_frozen.py. freeze_mapping() at lines 129-131 unconditionally constructs a new FrozenDict without checking whether the input is already a FrozenDict: `return FrozenDict({k: freeze(v) for k, v in value.items()})`. The new FrozenDict.__init__ sets _hash = None (line 36), discarding any cached hash from the original. By contrast, freeze() correctly short-circuits at line 92-93 with `if isinstance(value, FrozenDict): return value`. ProvenanceEntry.__post_init__ (provenance.py:66) calls freeze_mapping(self.payload), and the pattern recurs in at least 12 other __post_init__ methods across the codebase. The finding's line number (50) is wrong — line 50 is __hash__, not freeze_mapping (which is at line 129) — but the described behavior is accurate. The O(N*depth) claim is somewhat overstated: inner FrozenDict values survive with cached hashes because freeze() is idempotent, so only the top-level FrozenDict wrapper loses its _hash. Nevertheless, unnecessary allocation and a top-level hash cache eviction occur every time an already-frozen FrozenDict is re-frozen through freeze_mapping(). This is a genuine production-readiness concern in a hot path. Fix: add `if isinstance(value, FrozenDict): return value` at the start of freeze_mapping().

verdict_b.reasoning:
The code defect exists as described. freeze_mapping() at line 129-131 of /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/_frozen.py unconditionally creates a new FrozenDict — including when the input is already a FrozenDict — setting _hash = None and discarding any cached hash. In contrast, freeze() has an explicit early-return guard at lines 92-93 (if isinstance(value, FrozenDict): return value) that makes it idempotent. freeze_mapping() lacks this guard entirely.

ProvenanceEntry.__post_init__ (provenance.py line 66) calls freeze_mapping(self.payload) unconditionally. If a FrozenDict is passed as payload — which is valid since the payload field is typed as Mapping[str, Any] — a new FrozenDict wrapper is created with _hash=None, discarding any previously computed hash.

The severity concern is real but partially overstated: the main hot path in ProvenanceTrail.record() builds payload from **kwargs (plain dict), and from_records() deserializes from plain dicts, so neither routine re-freezes an already-frozen FrozenDict in practice. However, freeze_mapping() is used in many __post_init__ methods across the codebase (claims.py, schema.py, fence.py, hypothesis.py, verdict.py, determinism.py, toolkit.py, seam.py), and any of these that receive an already-frozen FrozenDict will silently drop the cached hash. The fix is straightforward: add an isinstance(value, FrozenDict) early-return to freeze_mapping(), mirroring what freeze() already does. The PoC is reproducible from the code as written — calling freeze_mapping(existing_frozen_dict) demonstrably returns a new object with _hash=None.

all_results.title: Provenance chain full re-verification at end of every run — O(N) redundant SHA-256/HMAC recomputes

The ProvenanceTrail is purely in-memory and maintains the chain invariant during record() calls (line 144 already computes and stores the digest), so re-verifying all N entries at run end is entirely redundant under normal (non-tampered) execution. all_results.title: Connection pool max_size hardcoded with no env-var override
all_results.suggested_fix: Point at TCP black-hole. get_checkpointer() hangs for up to 30s before raising.

verdict_a.reasoning: The code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py lines 244-251 constructs a ConnectionPool with hardcoded min_size=1, max_size=8 and no connect_timeout in the kwargs dict. There is no ASSAY_POOL_MAX_SIZE env var or any other runtime override for the pool size anywhere in the file. The kwargs dict (line 248-249) contains only autocommit, prepare_threshold, and row_factory — no connect_timeout. With open=True (line 250), the pool opens connections immediately during get_checkpointer() construction. Against a TCP black-hole host, this will block for the OS-level TCP timeout (often 20-30+ seconds) before raising, exactly as the PoC describes. Both sub-issues (hardcoded max_size with no env override, and missing connect_timeout) are confirmed present in the current code. This is a genuine production-readiness concern: operators cannot tune pool size without a code change, and a misconfigured or temporarily unreachable DB endpoint will hang the caller for an unbounded period.

verdict_b.reasoning:
The code at lines 244-251 of /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py confirms both sub-issues described in the finding:

1. Hardcoded pool sizing: `ConnectionPool` is constructed with literal `min_size=1, max_size=8`. There is no `ASSAY_POOL_MAX_SIZE` env-var lookup anywhere in the file. The only env-var the module reads are `ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL`, `ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS`, and `ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND` — none govern pool sizing.

2. No connect_timeout: The `kwargs` dict passed to the pool (line 248) contains only `autocommit`, `prepare_threshold`, and `row_factory`. No `connect_timeout` or `options` setting a statement/tcp timeout is present.

The PoC is reproducible: `open=True` (line 250) causes `ConnectionPool.__init__` to synchronously open `min_size` connections. Against a TCP black-hole there is no application-level timeout to bound that wait, so the call hangs until the OS TCP timeout fires (often 2+ minutes, not merely 30s — the finding understates the potential hang duration). This is a genuine production-readiness concern: the factory blocks the calling thread with no bound, and operators have no env-var knob to tune pool concurrency for the 8-worker reasoning pool.

all_results.title: Module-level ThreadPoolExecutor _pool has no atexit shutdown

all_results.title: Gate node re-entry can double-call recorder for same decision
all_results.suggested_fix: Wire two parallel gate nodes sharing same ProvenanceTrail recorder. Both call recorder after getting approved. Trail receives two "gate" entries with same gate name.

verdict_a.reasoning:
The code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py is structured so that each gate node is built with a specific Gate object (with a unique gate.name). The already_decided guard at line 109 uses gate.name to check for terminal decisions in state. The recorder is an optional callback closure captured per node, not shared by default.

The PoC scenario requires "two parallel gate nodes sharing same ProvenanceTrail recorder" AND the same gate name — which is an explicit misconfiguration (two nodes wrapping the same Gate object or same gate name). In normal usage, each call to make_gate_node produces a node for a distinct gate; different gate names mean already_decided checks are independent and recorder calls are for different logical decisions, not duplicates.

Additionally, LangGraph's execution model does not expose true intra-run concurrency on a single checkpoint thread in a way that would allow two invocations of the same node function to race inside a single graph step. The interrupt() call at line 123 is a LangGraph-internal suspend/resume mechanism, not a multi-threaded concurrent primitive. The re-entry scenario the docstring guards against (lines 13-24) is checkpoint replay (the same node being re-entered on resume), which the already_decided check at line 109 correctly handles by returning {} without re-calling recorder.

The described double-call race is not reproducible from the code as written under normal wiring. The finding is speculative about a misconfiguration scenario (sharing recorder + gate name across two nodes) that the API does not encourage and that LangGraph's execution model does not naturally produce.

verdict_b.reasoning:
The code does not exhibit the described vulnerability. The gate node at gatenode.py lines 108-132 already has a terminal-decision idempotency guard: `already_decided(state, gate.name)` at line 109 returns `{}` immediately (without calling `recorder`) if the gate already holds an approved decision in state. This directly prevents double-recording for the same gate.

The PoC scenario ("two parallel gate nodes sharing same ProvenanceTrail recorder") does not produce the claimed duplicate. In LangGraph, `interrupt()` suspends the entire node — execution within a node is sequential, not parallel. Two parallel gate nodes in a LangGraph graph would be two separate node closures with distinct `gate` objects (different gate names). Each would call `recorder` once for its own gate name, producing two entries for two different gates — not duplicate entries for the same gate.

For the same gate to be double-recorded, the same node would need to be entered twice, which is exactly what `already_decided` prevents. ProvenanceTrail.record() is indeed non-idempotent (it appends unconditionally), but the architectural guard at line 109 prevents the double-entry path the PoC requires. The finding conflates "ProvenanceTrail is non-idempotent" with "double-call will occur," but the code prevents the double-call at the gate node level. The PoC is not reproducible from the current code as written.

all_results.title: Provenance trail is unkeyed (forgery-vulnerable) by default when secret=None
all_results.suggested_fix: Call run_study without secret. Edit entry 1 payload. Recompute chain from genesis. verify_records(entries) with secret=None passes.

verdict_a.reasoning: The code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py line 255 confirms `secret: bytes | None = None`. When secret=None, ProvenanceTrail uses plain SHA-256 (provenance.py lines 89-90). verify_records() with secret=None recomputes SHA-256 directly from each entry's fields without any MAC. An adversary with control over serialized entries can edit any payload, recompute entry_hash values forward from genesis using the same SHA-256 logic, and produce a forged chain that passes verify_records(). The module docstring (provenance.py lines 18-23) explicitly documents this limitation ("not forgery-proof"), confirming the behavior is intentional by design — but there is no runtime warning emitted when run_study is called without a secret, so production callers can unknowingly use the insecure default. The PoC is reproducible from the actual code as written.

verdict_b.reasoning: The issue exists exactly as described. In /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py line 255, `run_study` accepts `secret: bytes | None = None` with `None` as the default. In provenance.py, the `_digest` function (lines 69-91) branches on `secret is None`: when None it uses plain `hashlib.sha256`, otherwise HMAC-SHA256. The `verify_records` function (lines 222-243) recomputes with the same `_digest` call, so a chain produced with `secret=None` and then forged (payload edited, full SHA-256 chain recomputed from genesis) will pass `verify_records(entries, secret=None)` without error. The module docstring (lines 18-23) explicitly acknowledges this limitation ("it is NOT forgery-proof"), but this documentation is inside the provenance module, not surfaced to callers at runtime. There is no warning, deprecation notice, or log message when `run_study` is called without a secret. The PoC is reproducible from the current code as written. This is a genuine production-readiness concern: any deployment that relies on provenance trail verification for compliance/governance without supplying a secret has a forgeable audit trail, and the API design makes the insecure path the default path.

all_results.title: Unclosed file handle in license_gate.py
all_results.suggested_fix: In constrained environment, leaked file descriptors exhaust process limit.

verdict_a.reasoning: Line 19 of /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/license_gate.py contains exactly the pattern described: `packages = json.load(open(path, encoding="utf-8"))`. The file object is created inline and passed directly to `json.load()` with no context manager (`with` statement) and no explicit `.close()` call. The file handle is left open until CPython's reference-counting GC collects it (immediate in CPython on function exit, but not guaranteed in other runtimes or under exception paths). The PoC is reproducible from the current code as written — it has not been fixed in any prior hardening pass. In production environments with strict file descriptor limits or non-CPython runtimes, this is a genuine resource-leak concern. The fix is trivial: wrap with `with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as fh: packages = json.load(fh)`.

verdict_b.reasoning: Line 19 of /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/license_gate.py reads: packages = json.load(open(path, encoding="utf-8")). The file is opened without a context manager, so the handle is not explicitly closed. In CPython the handle is closed promptly via reference counting, but this is an implementation detail not guaranteed by the language spec. Under PyPy or other GC-based runtimes, or if json.load raises before the anonymous file object is collected, the descriptor leaks until GC runs. The issue exists exactly as described and has not been fixed by prior hardening passes.

all_results.title: Bearer token scrubbing regex misses base64 characters (+ and /) — partial redaction leaves token fragments
all_results.suggested_fix: [REDACTED]: retains +iOiJIUzI1NiJ9.payload/signature after redaction.

verdict_a.reasoning: Line 39 of /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/capture_transcripts.py contains: re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-]{20,}"). The character class [A-Za-z0-9._\-] omits + and /, which are standard base64 alphabet characters. JWTs and many OAuth bearer tokens encode their payload in base64url or standard base64 and frequently contain these characters. The regex match stops at the first + or / encountered, so the portion of the token after that character is left unredacted in the captured transcript. The PoC is directly reproducible from the code as written: a token like eyJhbGc+iOiJIUzI1NiJ9.payload/signature would result in only "Bearer eyJhbGc" being replaced with [REDACTED], leaving "+iOiJIUzI1NiJ9.payload/signature" exposed. This is a genuine production-readiness concern because transcripts are committed to the repo by the pre-commit hook, and partial token fragments can still be exploitable or used to reconstruct secrets. The issue does not appear to have been fixed in prior hardening passes — the pattern is still literally [A-Za-z0-9._\-]{20,} with no + or / included.

verdict_b.reasoning: Line 39 of /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/capture_transcripts.py contains the pattern `re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-]{20,}")`. The character class `[A-Za-z0-9._\-]` excludes `+` and `/`, both of which are valid base64 characters that appear in JWTs and standard base64-encoded bearer tokens. The PoC is reproducible and the behavior is actually worse than described: if the base64 token segment before the first `+` or `/` is fewer than 20 characters (common in JWTs where `eyJhbGc` is only 7 chars before a `+`), the entire token fails to match the `{20,}` minimum and is left completely unredacted in the transcript. This is a genuine production-readiness concern because these transcripts are committed to the repo by the pre-commit hook and could expose OAuth/JWT bearer tokens captured in agent session logs.
